source: Generated from a per-system Morphous manifest and prompt records. CSS variables are shadcn/tweakcn-compatible.
typography: Geist for refined hospitality product UI with Noto Sans JP guidance for Japanese headings, labels, body text, and numerals.
layout: Feather-soft panels, disciplined 8px radii, clear black-bill contrast actions, lake-blue focus rings, and spacious operations layouts for reservations, concierge, dining, and rooms.

prompts.label: Transparent swan feather motif source
prompts.prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: Morphous transparent motif cutout source
Primary request: Create only a swan feather motif with a subtle black swan bill accent, isolated full subject, no environment, no props, no text.
Subject: A single elegant white swan feather, long curved plume with crisp barbs and soft down at the base, pearlescent white and warm ivory feather detail, a small refined black bill-shaped contrast accent integrated near the quill base like a minimal natural marker. Hospitality design system mood: calm, refined, graceful, premium, precise.
Composition/framing: centered complete cutout with generous padding; do not crop the feather tip, barbs, down, or quill. Keep the feather fully separated from the background.
Lighting/mood: clean studio light that preserves white feather detail, soft lake-blue reflected shadowing, elegant hotel-lobby calm, no cast shadow.
Transparent workflow: place the subject on a perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background for removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, or lighting variation. Do not use #ff00ff anywhere in the subject.
Constraints: no whole swan body, no lake scene, no water ripples, no second feather, no cartoon mascot, no text, no watermark, no decorative background.

prompts.label: Light-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous light-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate a comprehensive light-mode design-system board for a Morphous theme named "Morphous Swan" derived from an elegant white swan feather motif.
Reference use: Extract palette, texture rhythm, contrast, spacing mood, and interaction language from a white swan feather with a black bill accent and lake-blue reflections. The board must visibly respect these motif colors: white feather, pearlescent ivory, black bill contrast, lake blue, refined silver neutrals, and deep ink.
Canvas: exact wide 16:9, target 3840x2160 high-resolution source board.
Resolution and legibility: 4K-class source board with large readable labels; avoid dense tiny text. Make all section titles and token names legible in gallery preview.
Required visible sections: Morphous Swan title, motif story, Palette, Typography, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Dashboard/Data, Forms, Table, Command/Search, Status States, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadow, Tokens, Sample Usage, Generated Assets.
Typography: include English and Japanese examples with Geist and Noto Sans JP guidance for elegant hospitality product UI; use calm, clear labels and no long paragraphs.
UI coverage: buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, table rows, search command, sidebar navigation, reservation metric cards, occupancy line chart, revenue bar chart, guest mix donut chart, empty/loading/error states, spacing, radius, border, shadow, texture samples.
Style/medium: premium shadcn/tweakcn-style product design system, realistic UI mockup, precise grid, 8px card radius, feather-soft surfaces, high-contrast black bill details, lake-blue focus rings, refined hospitality dashboard for bookings, concierge, dining, and rooms.
Color palette: use only motif-derived colors and near-neutrals: feather white, pearl ivory, mist silver, lake blue, glacial blue, black bill, ink navy, reed gray, soft porcelain, calm aqua.
Text: short readable UI labels; include English and Japanese guidance labels such as "English UI", "日本語UI", "Noto Sans JP", "Reservations", "Occupancy", "Guest profile", "Suite ready", "Concierge notes".
Constraints: no unrelated bright colors, no generic purple gradients, no noisy lake scene, no random brand logos, no watermark, no misspelling of "Morphous Swan".

prompts.label: Dark-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous dark-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate a separate dark-mode version of the Morphous Swan design-system board. It must feel like the same elegant hospitality system translated to dark mode, not a new theme.
Reference use: Preserve the white swan feather motif, black bill contrast accents, lake-blue focus states, refined neutrals, feather-soft texture, hospitality dashboard workflow, Japanese typography guidance, and shadcn/tweakcn component coverage from the light Morphous Swan board.
Canvas: exact wide 16:9, target 3840x2160 high-resolution source board.
Resolution and legibility: 4K-class source board with large readable labels; avoid dense tiny text. Make section titles, palette names, token labels, and component labels legible in gallery preview.
Required visible sections: Morphous Swan, Dark Mode, motif story, Palette, Typography, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Dashboard/Data, Forms, Table, Command/Search, Status States, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadow, Tokens, Sample Usage, Generated Assets.
Visual details: deep ink-navy and moonlit lake-blue background, charcoal glass panels, feather-white text and surfaces, black bill contrast used for premium controls, lake-blue rings and selected states, pearl ivory highlights, mist-silver dividers, subtle swan-feather texture overlays, crisp accessible card borders, calm luxury-hotel operations dashboard.
UI coverage: buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, table rows, command/search, sidebar navigation, reservation metric cards, occupancy line chart, revenue bar chart, guest mix donut chart, empty/loading/error states, spacing, radius, border, shadow, texture samples.
Typography: include English and Japanese examples with Geist and Noto Sans JP guidance, using short readable labels such as "English UI", "日本語UI", "Reservations", "Occupancy", "Suite ready", "Concierge notes", "Guest profile".
Constraints: no neon overload, no generic purple gradients, no pure-black-only flat background, no unrelated saturated colors, no noisy lake scene, no random brand logos, no watermark, no misspelling of "Morphous Swan".
